summary: Five-time world champions and clear group favourites under Carlo Ancelotti, looking to better their 2018 and 2022 quarter-final exits. A star-studded, fluid front line led by Vinícius Jr, with Casemiro a major set-piece threat. Dreaming of emulating their 1994 USA triumph.
style: In possession a dynamic 3-2-4-1 — the right-back pushes on, Bruno Guimarães, Casemiro and a dropping Cunha progress the ball, and the front line (Vinícius, Raphinha, Cunha, Martinelli) interchanges constantly with wide rotations. Vinícius starts centrally but roams to his favoured left. Out of possession, a high press from a 4-4-2.

counterReliance.note: Fluid possession and rotations; devastating breaking at speed

strengths: Elite, interchanging front line (Vinícius, Raphinha, Cunha, Martinelli) with positional fluidity
Guimarães' line-splitting passing into the forwards' runs
Casemiro a serious set-piece threat — 8 set-piece goals for Man Utd this season, the most of any player in Europe's top-five leagues

keyPlayers.name: Vinícius Jr
keyPlayers.note: Leads the line but roams left to attack 1v1 — 7.7 take-ons per 90, 7th-highest in Europe's top-five leagues; a nightmare matchup

keyPlayers.name: Matheus Cunha
keyPlayers.note: Drops to progress and create, opening space for the rotations around him

keyPlayers.name: Casemiro
keyPlayers.note: Progression anchor and the dead-ball threat — Europe's top-five-league set-piece scorer this season

tactics: Rotate the forwards and full-backs to pull defenders out of position, then release runners behind with Guimarães' passing; press high to win it back early. Neymar has been recalled and will hope to feature, most likely from the bench. The depth of attacking options is the headline.
notes: Won more World Cups than any nation (5); chasing a first since 2002.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group C (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
